title: Web Developer
description:
**About The Company**

The Web Developer will collaborate closely with cross\\-functional teams, including UI/UX designers, operations, and business stakeholders, to deliver high\\-quality, scalable, and efficient web solutions that align with our business objectives. A solid understanding of databases, code versioning tools such as Git or SVN, and web server management (e.g., Apache, Nginx) is essential. A bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent working experience, is required. Candidates should have a foundational understanding of software development lifecycles, UI/UX principles, and modern deployment practices like CI/CD pipelines. Experience with cloud services such as AWS, Azure, or DigitalOcean, as well as familiarity with OCR frameworks, will be considered advantageous. Additionally, strong problem\\-solving skills, the ability to work independently and collaboratively, and excellent communication skills are vital for success in this role.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Develop, automate, and maintain web applications and services to support business and operational goals while adhering to performance and quality code best practices.
* Work with team leads and members to identify requirements, define project scope, and ensure deadlines and objectives are met.
* Collaborate with UI/UX teams to ensure visual and functional consistency across applications.
* Document product specifications, technical requirements, and project progress to facilitate clear communication and future reference.
* Stay updated on emerging web development trends, tools, and technologies to continuously enhance skill set and project quality.
* Engage with operational teams to understand business workflows and tailor web solutions accordingly.
* Participate in system testing, automated testing, and deployment processes to ensure robust and reliable applications.
* Assist in troubleshooting and resolving technical issues swiftly to minimize operational disruptions.


**Benefits**
 Allied Container Group offers a competitive benefits package including comprehensive health insurance, opportunities for professional development, and a collaborative work environment. Employees will have access to cutting\\-edge technology and the chance to work on innovative projects within a global logistics organization. Additionally, the company supports career growth and mobility, with potential relocation opportunities to Dubai or Oman after successful completion of the initial probation period. We value work\\-life balance and provide a supportive environment that encourages continuous learning and development.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 Allied Container Group is committed to creating an inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe in fostering diversity and inclusion across all levels of our organization, ensuring all employees are valued and empowered to contribute their best.

title: Software Developer
description:

Our team comprises talented professionals who are passionate about leveraging the latest technologies to solve complex business challenges.  We are seeking a Full Stack Software Developer specialized in ASP.NET MVC, SQL Server, and React to join our dynamic development team.  The ideal candidate should possess between 3 to 6 years of relevant experience in software development, with a proven track record in building enterprise\\-level web applications. Familiarity with RESTful API design, version control systems like Git, and CI/CD pipelines is essential. Exposure to cloud services such as AWS and databases like MongoDB will be considered a plus. **Responsibilities**

title: Software Engineer
description:
**About The Company**
 Lucidity is a rapidly growing technology company specializing in innovative cloud storage solutions. Recognized for its first\\-of\\-its\\-kind product, Lucidity offers a transformative approach to cloud storage management by providing automated, cost\\-effective, and scalable storage optimization across multiple cloud platforms including AWS, Azure, and GCP. The company's cutting\\-edge technology addresses over\\-provisioned storage issues by dynamically adjusting capacity, thereby maximizing disk utilization and significantly reducing costs for enterprise clients. Backed by prominent investors such as Alpha Wave Global, WestBridge Capital, and Beenext with a funding of $32 million, Lucidity has established itself as a leader in cloud storage optimization. The company has received accolades like the Best Infra and Dev Tools SaaS Startup by SaaSBoomi and has been listed among India’s Top Startups by LinkedIn. With a presence across India, Abu Dhabi, the US, and the UK, Lucidity is committed to reshaping the future of cloud storage through innovation and excellence.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will play a crucial role in ensuring the quality and reliability of our cloud storage optimization platform. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work on complex, large\\-scale systems in a fast\\-paced environment. As an SDET at Lucidity, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining automated test frameworks, executing comprehensive test plans, and collaborating closely with the engineering teams to deliver high\\-quality software products. Your expertise will help us maintain our standards of excellence, improve our testing processes, and ensure our solutions meet the demanding needs of enterprise clients. The role is based in Bangalore or remote, with the first month of onboarding onsite and one week onsite per quarter thereafter.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 The ideal candidate should possess a strong technical background with a minimum of 2 to 8 years of experience in software testing and automation. A degree in Computer Science or a related field (B.Tech/M.Tech) from a reputed college is preferred. Candidates should have proven experience working with automation frameworks, cloud platforms, and API testing. Strong coding skills in Java are essential, along with experience in automation tools and frameworks such as Selenium, Playwright, TestNG, and Pytest. Familiarity with cloud environments like AWS and Azure, as well as Linux, MySQL, and version control systems like Git, is required. The candidate should demonstrate excellent analytical skills, problem\\-solving abilities, and a passion for quality assurance and automation. Knowledge of Spring Boot, Docker, Postman, JMeter, and Wiremock is considered a plus.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 The SDET will be responsible for creating and maintaining automated test cases, executing test suites, and diagnosing reported bugs to ensure the overall quality of our platform. Key responsibilities include designing, developing, and troubleshooting automated testing frameworks within a continuous integration environment. The candidate will work closely with software developers and cross\\-functional teams to derive testing requirements, reproduce and debug issues, and verify fixes. You will be expected to develop detailed test plans, test cases, and test reports, ensuring comprehensive coverage of complex API and web\\-based applications that interface with database back\\-ends. Additionally, the role involves estimating testing tasks, working independently with minimal supervision, and contributing to process improvements to enhance testing efficiency and effectiveness.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Lucidity, we prioritize our employees' growth and well\\-being. We offer a high\\-growth work environment that encourages innovation and autonomy. Our benefits include flexible working hours, remote work options, unlimited leave policy, and opportunities for professional development. Employees are also invited to participate in company offsites and team\\-building activities that foster a collaborative and inclusive culture. Working at Lucidity provides a chance to be part of a pioneering organization in deep tech, with ample opportunities to contribute to impactful projects and accelerate your career trajectory.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 Lucidity is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that values diversity and equal opportunity. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that diverse teams drive innovation and excellence, and we are dedicated to providing a workplace where everyone can thrive and contribute meaningfully to our mission of transforming cloud storage management.

title: Product & Engineering Interns
description:
**About Planora** 




 Planora is solving one of travel's biggest unsolved problems: group coordination. Most group trips never happen—they die in endless group chats, indecision, and flaky commitments. We're building a commitment\\-first platform that uses AI, behavioral design, and smart deposit mechanisms to ensure trips actually happen.
 



 We're an early\\-stage startup building in India, and we're looking for interns who want real startup experience—not just another line on their resume.
 



  





**What You'll Do** 




 This isn't a typical "shadow and learn" internship. You'll work directly with our founding team leads and own real product features from concept to launch.
 



**Depending on your role, you might:** 



* **Backend Engineering:** 
 Build and ship API endpoints, database schemas, or backend services
* **Flutter Development:** 
 Develop cross\\-platform mobile features using Flutter
* **Frontend Engineering:** 
 Create responsive web interfaces and user flows
* **Full Stack Development:** 
 Own full features end\\-to\\-end across the stack
* **ML/Data Science:** 
 Build recommendation systems, prediction models, or data pipelines
* **UI/UX Design:** 
 Conduct user research, design wireframes, and create visual interfaces
* **Product Management:** 
 Define product roadmaps, write user stories, and coordinate across teams



  





**What You'll Learn** 



* How early\\-stage startups operate (product decisions, resource constraints, iteration speed)
* Cross\\-functional collaboration between engineering, design, and product
* How to ship fast with limited resources
* Real\\-world problem\\-solving beyond textbook knowledge
* Direct mentorship from people who've built products before



  





**What We're Looking For** 




**Required:** 



* Foundational knowledge in your domain (we'll help you level up)
* Self\\-driven work ethic—you don't wait to be told what to do
* Ability to learn quickly and adapt
* Good communication skills
* Availability for at least 3 months



**Bonus points for:** 



* Previous projects (personal, academic, or professional)
* GitHub contributions or portfolio work
* Experience with our tech stack (Next.js, Python, Flutter, PostgreSQL, AWS)
* Genuine interest in travel, AI, or behavioral product design



**You're NOT a fit if:** 



* You need constant hand\\-holding
* You're only looking for a certificate or easy recommendation letter
* You expect a structured corporate training program
* You're not comfortable with ambiguity and fast changes



  





**Compensation \\& Benefits** 




 Let's be transparent: this is an unpaid internship.
 



 We're a bootstrapped early\\-stage startup. We can't offer a stipend right now, but here's what we do offer:
 



 ✅
 **Real product ownership** 
 – Your code/designs will be in production, used by real users
 



 ✅
 **Direct mentorship** 
 – Weekly 1:1s with founding leads in your domain
 



 ✅
 **Portfolio\\-worthy work** 
 – Ship features you can showcase in interviews
 



 ✅
 **Cross\\-functional exposure** 
 – Attend product, design, and tech discussions
 



 ✅
 **Startup learning** 
 – See fundraising, GTM strategy, user research firsthand
 



 ✅
 **Flexible schedule** 
 – Work around your college/commitments
 



 ✅
 **Remote\\-first** 
 – Work from anywhere in India
 



**Future potential:** 
 High\\-performing interns may be considered for paid roles or founding team positions as we grow and raise funding.
 



  





**Our Culture** 




 We're building a culture of:
 


* **Builders over talkers** 
 – We ship, iterate, and learn
* **Ownership over tasks** 
 – You own outcomes, not just outputs
* **Speed over perfection** 
 – Done is better than perfect
* **Learning over knowing** 
 – We don't expect you to know everything
* **Transparency** 
 – We share our wins, losses, and learnings openly



  





**How to Apply** 




 Email
 




 keshav@planoratrips.in \\| somu@planoratrips.in
 
 with:
 



**Subject:** 
 "Internship Application \\- \\[Your Role]"
 



**Include:** 



1. Role(s) you're interested in
2. Why this problem/product excites you (3\\-4 lines)
3. Link to something you've built (GitHub, Figma, portfolio, project report)
4. Your availability (hours/week, duration)



**Timeline:** 



* Initial screening: 3\\-5 days
* Quick call: 20\\-30 min with the relevant lead
* Small task: Practical assignment (2\\-3 hours max)
* Decision: Within a week



 We move fast. If you want to learn by doing and ship real features, apply now.

* Participate actively in the design phase, contributing ideas and expertise to create scalable, efficient, and innovative software architectures.
* Mentor and support junior team members, fostering a collaborative and knowledge\\-sharing environment.
* Implement high\\-quality logic, ensuring seamless integration with external systems via diverse APIs.

* Design, develop, and refine testing tools and development support utilities to streamline processes and address development challenges.
* Integrate with configuration management and update tools to ensure smooth deployment and maintenance of software solutions.
* Collaborate with cross\\-functional teams to understand requirements and deliver solutions aligned with business goals.


**Benefits**

* Flexible working arrangements, including hybrid work options combining office and remote work.
* Work alongside experienced researchers and product developers in a collaborative and innovative setting.
* Competitive salary package with above\\-market standards.
* Participation in ESOP programs, enabling employees to share in the company's growth and success.

 Equal Opportunity
   

  

 DRIMCO GmbH is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, age, religion, disability, or sexual orientation. We believe that diversity enhances innovation and creativity, and we strive to provide a supportive environment where all employees can thrive and contribute to our mission.

title: LEAD ENGINEER
description:
At OakNorth, we’re on a mission to empower the UK’s most ambitious businesses. Since 2015, we’ve lent over $21 billion across the UK and US, helped create more than 58,000 new homes and 36,000 new jobs, and supported hundreds of thousands of personal savers — all while fuelling the UK economy.
   

  

 OakNorth is a profitable business that has supported the growth of thousands of entrepreneurs with a new standard of business lending. We're looking for people to join our Engineering team in building upon this success, as we grow to play a key part in redefining the future of business banking with next generation financial tools and products.
   

  

 This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast\\-paced, growing bank with a reputation for doing things differently. We don’t want another cog in the machine, we’re looking for self\\-starters and bold thinkers who want to pave their own career paths.
   

  

**The role 👋**
* Have a strong technical background, including 9\\+ years of experience designing and engineering large scale systems
* Strong experience in either backend (Python) or frontend (React) in enterprise\\-class applications, with a pragmatic attitude to tech and a willingness to grow into full stack over time
* Architecture and system design knowledge (prefer in Domain\\-driven Design, Event design)
* Measure your success in terms of business impact, not lines of code
* Internalise the best ideas from across the organisation, humbly setting a vision that others can get behind
* Embrace DevOps culture: You build it, you run it
* Work well cross\\-functionally and earn trust from co\\-workers at all levels
* Care deeply about mentorship and growing your colleagues
* Prefer simple solutions and designs over complex ones
* Enjoy working with a diverse group of people with different areas of expertise.
* You challenge the existing approach when you see the cliff edge racing towards us, but also get on board once the options have been debated and the team has made a decision
* You're comfortably organised amongst chaos
* You’re a broad thinker and have the capability to see the potential impact of decisions across the wider business
* Own the technical direction and act as the go\\-to architect for your area for a domain or product area, and bring engineers, PMs, and stakeholders along with you
* Raise the engineering bar:
* Champion design quality, reliability, observability and security
* Challenge over\\-complicated designs and push for pragmatic, evolvable solutions
* Coach and mentor Senior and mid\\-level engineers, giving clear feedback and helping them grow into broader responsibilities
* Partner closely with Product and Delivery to:
* Shape roadmaps, Highlight risks early
* Make sensible trade\\-offs between scope, risk, and timelines
* Take ownership during incidents and tricky production problems, coordinating technical response and follow\\-ups, not just writing the fix


**Technology 🧱**
* We're pragmatic about our technology choices. These are some of the things we use at the moment:
* 🗃 Python
* 🗄️ PostgreSQL, BigQuery, MySQL
* 🎨 TypeScript, React, styled\\-components
* 🔧 Jest, React Testing Library, Cypress, pytest
* ☁️ AWS, GCP
* 🚀 ECS Fargate, Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions


**How We Expect You To Work 👷‍♀️**
* We expect you to work in these ways, as well as encouraging and enabling these practices from others:
* Collaborate 💬 \\- We work in cross\\-functional, mission driven, autonomous squads that gel over time. We pair program to work better through shared experience and knowledge.
* Focus on outcomes over outputs ✅ \\- Solving a problem for users that translates to business results is our goal. Measurements focused on that goal help us to understand if we are succeeding.
* Practice continuous improvement 💡 \\- We optimise for feedback now, rather than presume what might be needed in the future and introduce complexity before it will be used. This means we learn faster. We share learnings in blame\\-free formats, so that we do not repeat things that have failed, but still have confidence to innovate.
* Seek to understand our users 👂 \\- We constantly seek understanding from data and conversations to better serve our users' needs, taking an active part in research to hear from them directly and regularly.
* Embrace and enable continuous deployment 🤖 \\- Seamless delivery of changes into an environment \\- without manual intervention \\- is essential for us to ensure that we are highly productive; consider resiliency; and practice security by design.
* Test outside\\-in, test first 🚦 \\- TDD keeps us confident in moving fast, and deploying regularly. We want to solve user problems, and so we test with that mindset \\- writing scenarios first, then considering our solution; coupling tests to behaviour, rather than implementation.
* You build it, you run it ⚙️ \\- We embrace DevOps culture and end\\-to\\-end ownership of products and features. Every engineer, regardless of their role, has the opportunity to lead delivery of features from start to finish.
* Be cloud native ☁️ \\- We leverage automation and hosted services to deliver resilient, secure services quickly and consistently. Where SaaS tools help us achieve more productivity and better\\-quality results for a cheap price, we use these to automate low value tasks.


**How We Expect You To Behave ❤️**
* We embrace differences and know that when we can be ourselves at work, we are happier, more motivated, and creative. We want to be able to bring our whole selves to work, have our own perspectives and know that we belong. As such, through your behaviours at work, we expect you to reflect and actively sustain a healthy engineering environment that looks like this:
* A wide range of voices heard to the benefit of all
* Teams that are clearly happy, engaged, and laugh together
* Perceivable safety to have an opinion or ask a question
* No egos \\- people listen to and learn from others at all levels, with strong opinions held loosely


**Benefits \\& Perks 😍**
* Health Insurance for employee and dependents.
* Wellbeing and social events
* Support causes that matter to you – Volunteering time off


**About Us**
 We’re OakNorth Bank and we embolden entrepreneurs to realise their ambitions, understand their markets, and apply data intelligence to everyday decisions to scale successfully at pace.
   

  

 Banking should be barrier\\-free. It’s a belief at our very core, inspired by our entrepreneurial spirit, driven by the unmet financial needs of millions, and delivered by our data\\-driven tools.
   

  

 And for those who love helping businesses thrive? Our savings accounts help diversify the high street and create new jobs, all while earning savers some of the highest interest on the market.
   

  

 But we go beyond finance, to empower our people, encourage professional growth and create an environment where everyone can thrive. We strive to create an inclusive and diverse workplace where people can be themselves and succeed.
   

  

***Our story***
 OakNorth Bank was built on the foundations of frustrations with old\\-school banking. In 2005, when our founders tried to get capital for their data analytics company, the computer said ‘no’. Unfortunately, all major banks in the UK were using the same computer – and it was broken.
   

  

 Why was it so difficult for a profitable business with impressive cashflow, retained clients, and clear commercial success to get a loan?
   

  

 The industry was backward\\-looking and too focused on historic financials, rather than future potential.
   

  

 So, what if there was a bank, founded by entrepreneurs, for entrepreneurs? One that offered a dramatically better borrowing experience for businesses?
   

  

 No more what ifs, OakNorth Bank exists.

description:
**This job is with Amazon, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
**Description**
 If you are looking for an opportunity to solve deep technical problems and build innovative solutions in a fast paced environment working with smart, passionate software developers, this might be the role for you.
   

  

 Within the Transportation Technology and Services organization, Relay Central delivers a seamless, intelligent, and trusted foundation that empowers and enables Amazon's middle mile carriers to thrive in a fast\\-moving, complex environment. We believe that great products and features should be an enabler and let carriers focus on what matters most \\- safety, reliability, and performance on the road while delivering for Amazon to grow their business. By unifying essential capabilities into one cohesive team and experience for carriers, we reduce friction, streamline decisions, and enable carriers to operate with confidence. Our vision is to be the backbone of Amazon Relay, built on thoughtful design, deep system integrations, and continuous innovation. We aim to simplify the complexity, surface the right insights at the right time, and scale the evolving needs of the network. Ultimately, Relay Central aspires to be the trust control tower for our carriers that unlock carrier potential to help shape the future of transportation.
   

  

 Relay Central creates a unified offering for carriers that enables smooth onboarding, effective trip management, and an exciting loyalty program experience, while upholding the highest standards for safety, compliance, trust, and delivery quality.
   

  

 Our team is actively seeking motivated and multi\\-talented individuals who are passionate about (re)inventing and transforming the transportation and logistics operations.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 You'll design and develop software used worldwide by our transportation partners and will regularly interact with engineering and business leadership. You will participate in the planning and execution of technology projects and operational excellence initiatives. You'll also mentor junior engineers in employing strong design and implementation practices.
   

  

 To accomplish this, you will be expected to have an established background in building scalable software systems, great communication skills, and a motivation to achieve results in a fast paced environment. You should be somebody who enjoys working on complex software, is customer\\-centric, and feels strongly about building good software as well as making that software achieve its operational goals. Experience with building and operating the web services backing mobile applications, especially at massive scale, will be very applicable and helpful.
   

  

 Come and see for yourself why we are one of the most admired companies in the US, rated \\#1 in Innovation and \\# 1 in Customer Service, and be part of our tradition of working hard, having fun and making history!
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 3\\+ years of non\\-internship professional software development experience
* 2\\+ years of non\\-internship design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
* Experience programming with at least one software programming language


**Preferred Qualifications**
* 3\\+ years of full software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations experience
* Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\\-we\\-hire/accommodations for more information. title: Senior Software Engineer II
description:
**This job is with Elsevier, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
**Senior Software Engineer\\-II**
**Would you like to be part of a team that delivers high\\-quality software to our customers?**
**Are you a highly visible champion with a 'can do' attitude and enthusiasm that inspires others?**
**About our Team:\\-**
 The Research Data Platform (RDP) is Elsevier's core data platform for research content and analytics. It provides high‑quality, well‑governed data about documents, works, people, organizations, funders, concepts and indicators, and the relationships between them. RDP ingests and normalises data from multiple sources, links and enriches that data, and exposes it through reliable services and data products that power flagship products such as Scopus, SciVal, Pure, Digital Commons and others. It combines product, engineering, operations and data science teams to deliver shared capabilities like data ingestion pipelines, matching and disambiguation, curation tools, analytics, and MLOps. RDP is organised into cross‑functional domain teams (for example Document, Sources, Works, Person, Organization, Funding, Concepts, Indicators and Consumption), supported by platform and foundations teams providing common infrastructure (AWS, Kafka, Databricks, DSM, Kubernetes), shared utilities and standards for data quality, observability and ways of working. Each team owns a set of services and data products end‑to‑end, from design and build through to operation and continuous improvement. For a Quality Engineer and a Developer, this means working in close partnership with product managers, data scientists and operations colleagues: designing and evolving services and pipelines, putting robust automated testing and quality gates around data and code, and helping ensure that RDP delivers reliable, scalable, secure data capabilities that other Elsevier products can safely build on.
   

  

**About the Role:\\-**
 This position performs complex research, design, and software development assignments within a software functional area or product line, and provides direct input to project plans, schedules, and methodology in the development of cross\\-functional software products. This position performs software design \\- typically across multiple systems; mentors more\\-junior members of the team; and talks to users/customers and translates their requests into solutions.
   

  

**Responsibilities:\\-**
* Interface with other technical personnel or team members to finalize requirements.
* Write and review portions of detailed specifications for the development of complex system components.
* Complete complex bug fixes.
* Work closely with other development team members to understand complex product requirements and translate them into software designs.
* Successfully implement development processes, coding best practices, and code reviews.
* Operate in various development environments (Agile, Waterfall, etc.) while collaborating with key stakeholders.
* Resolve complex technical issues as necessary.
* Train entry\\-level software engineers as directed by department management, ensuring they are knowledgeable in critical aspects of their roles.
* Keep abreast of new technology developments.
* Design and work with complex data models.
* Mentor less\\-senior software developers on development methodologies and optimization techniques.
* All other duties as assigned.


**Requirements:\\-**
 5\\+ years of Software Engineering experience
   

  

* BS Engineering/Computer Science or equivalent experience required
* Advanced knowledge of software development methodologies (e.g., Agile, Waterfall).
* Strong proficiency with data manipulation language including optimization techniques.
* Strong understanding of normalized/dimensional data modeling principles.
* Strong knowledge of multiple data storage subsystems.
* Expertise in development languages including but not limited to: Java/J2EE, JavaScript, JSP, C/C\\+\\+, HTML, XML, SQL, Windows, UNIX, and .Net.
* Strong research skills.
* Strong knowledge in industry best practices in development.
* Knowledge in using and developing applicable tool sets.
* Ability to interface competently with other technical personnel or team members to finalize requirements.
* Ability to work well with internal and external technology resources.
* Knowledge of test\\-driven development.
* Ability to write and review portions of detailed specifications for the development of complex system components.
* Ability to complete complex bug fixes.
* Good oral and written communications skills.


**Work In a Way That Works For You**
 We promote a healthy work/life balance and provide various well\\-being initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance, and sabbaticals to help you meet both your immediate responsibilities and long\\-term goals.
   

  

**Working For You**
 We offer comprehensive benefits to support your health and well\\-being, including:
   

  

* Health insurance for you and your family.
* Enhanced health insurance options at competitive rates.
* Group life insurance for financial security.
* Group accident insurance for protection against accidental death and permanent disability.
* Flexible working arrangements for work\\-life balance.
* Employee assistance programs for personal and work\\-related support.
* Medical screenings to prioritize your well\\-being.
* Modern family benefits, including maternity, paternity, and adoption support.
* Long\\-service awards to recognize dedication.
* New baby gifts to celebrate parenthood.
* Subsidized meals at specific locations.
* Various paid time\\-off options, including casual leave, sick leave, privilege leave, compassionate leave, special sick leave, and public holidays.
* Free transportation for home\\-office\\-home travel in select locations.


**About Business**
 We are a global leader in information and analytics, helping researchers and healthcare professionals' advance science and improve health outcomes. Building on our publishing heritage, we combine quality information and vast data sets with analytics to support visionary science, research, health education, interactive learning, and exceptional healthcare and clinical practice. At Elsevier, your work contributes to addressing the world's grand challenges and creating a more sustainable future. We harness innovative technologies to support science and healthcare, partnering for a better world.

title: Containers Engineer
description:
**This job is with Kyndryl, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
**Who We Are**
 At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission\\-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world's leading businesses. We are at the heart of progress; with proven expertise and a continuous flow of AI\\-powered insight, enabling smarter decisions, faster innovation, and a lasting competitive edge. For our people\\-Kyndryls\\-that means doing purposeful work that powers human progress. Join us and experience a flexible, supportive environment where your well\\-being is prioritized and your potential can thrive.
   

  

**The Role**
 Infrastructure Specialists at Kyndryl are project\\-based subject matter experts in all things infrastructure \\- good at providing analysis, documenting and diagraming work for hand\\-off, offering timely solutions, and generally "figuring it out." This is a hands\\-on role where your feel for the interaction between a system and its environment will be invaluable to every one of your clients.
   

  

**Job Description \\-**
**Container \\& Platform Engineering**
* Design, deploy, and manage Kubernetes clusters for enterprise workloads
* Package, deploy, and manage applications using Helm charts
* Implement best practices for container lifecycle management, scaling, and resiliency
* Troubleshoot container, pod, and cluster\\-level issues


**Storage \\& NetApp Integration**
* Integrate NetApp storage solutions with Kubernetes
+ Trident / Astra (stateful workloads)
+ Persistent Volumes (PV), StorageClasses

* Support NetApp ONTAP‑based storage for container platforms
* Optimize performance, availability, and data protection for stateful containers


**CI/CD \\& Git Pipelines**
* Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for containerized applications
* Implement Git‑based pipelines (e.g., GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps)
* Automate build, test, container image scanning, and deployment processes
* Enable GitOps practices for Kubernetes deployments


**Automation \\& Infrastructure as Code**
* Develop automation using scripts and declarative configurations
* Automate cluster provisioning, application deployment, and upgrades
* Create reusable templates for Helm charts and pipeline automation
* Support continuous improvement of automation frameworks


**Operations \\& Reliability**
* Monitor platform health, performance, and capacity
* Implement logging, monitoring, and alerting for containers
* Participate in incident management and root cause analysis
* Collaborate with DevOps, SRE, and application teams


 Your future at Kyndryl
   

  

 There are lots of opportunities to gain certification and qualifications on the job, and you'll continuously grow as a Cloud Hyperscaler. Many of our Infrastructure Specialists are on a path toward becoming either an Architect or Distinguished Engineer, and there are opportunities at every skill level to grow in either of these directions.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You're good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important \\- you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer\\-focused \\- someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you're open and borderless \\- naturally inclusive in how you work with others.
   

  

**Technical Skills**
**Required Skills \\& Qualifications**
* Overall 7\\+ years of experience into IT.
* Core Containerization Skills
* Strong experience with Docker (building, running, and managing containers)
* Writing and optimizing Dockerfiles for efficient image creation
* Managing container images (tagging, versioning, pushing/pulling from registries like Docker Hub, Harbor, ECR, ACR, GCR)
* Knowledge of OCI image standards and container lifecycle management
* Deep expertise in Kubernetes architecture and operations
* Strong hands‑on experience with NetApp storage for containers
* Container Orchestration
* Hands\\-on experience with Kubernetes (K8s) (deployments, services, pods, namespaces, ConfigMaps, secrets, etc.)
* Familiarity with Kubernetes distributions (OpenShift, EKS, AKS, GKE, Rancher)
* Experience with Helm charts for application packaging and deployment
* Scaling and load balancing containers in orchestrated environments
* Infrastructure \\& Cloud
* Deploying containers in cloud platforms (AWS ECS/EKS, Azure AKS, Google GKE)
* Familiarity with hybrid and multi\\-cloud container platforms
* Networking knowledge (CNI plugins, ingress controllers, service mesh like Istio/Linkerd)
* Persistent storage and volume management in containerized environments
* DevOps \\& Automation
* Building CI/CD pipelines for containerized applications (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI, ArgoCD, Tekton)
* Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible, Pulumi) for container deployments
* Automation of container build, scan, and deployment processes
* Knowledge of GitOps practices


**Automation \\& Tooling**
* YAML‑centric configuration and templating
* Scripting (Shell, Python, or equivalent)
* Exposure to Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible)
* Kubernetes CSI and storage lifecycle automation


**Platform \\& Tooling**
* Docker or container runtimes
* Git (branching and pull request workflows)
* Kubernetes storage concepts (PV, PVC, CSI)
* Exposure to cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP) is a plus


**Nice‑to‑Have Skills**
* GitOps tools (Argo CD, Flux)
* NetApp Astra / Trident
* Terraform or Ansible
* Observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana)
* Service mesh or API gateway exposure


**Soft Skills**
* Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills
* Ability to work in fast‑paced, production environments
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills
* Automation‑first and problem‑solving mindset


**Education**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience


**Certifications (Preferred)**
